resources.string: Higher values mean fewer false positives, lower values catch more.
Prioritizes detection accuracy using a larger AI model. Best for high-end devices.
Covers the exact shape of detected people instead of using rectangular boxes, so nearby objects won\'t be accidentally hidden. Uses more resources
Adjust the transparency of the cover.

Enable this ONLY if you selected \"Entire screen\" when starting capture. Uses motion tracking to reduce flickering, but may not work perfectly.\nIt is HIGHLY recommended to use \"Single app\" recording instead (available on Android 15+, and some devices on Android 14).
Notification permission is required for the service to run in the background
Please enable the accessibility service in Settings first
Preparing model. The first time may take a few minutes. Next times are usually faster.

KrazyHide uses this accessibility service to draw overlay content at full opacity. On Android 12 and above, regular overlays are limited to 80% opacity, which may not fully obscure detected content. Enabling this service allows KrazyHide to display the overlay at any opacity level you choose.\n\nScreen content is captured for on-device processing only. No data is sent anywhere — KrazyHide does not have internet permission.
KrazyHide needs a few permissions to work

Required so KrazyHide can place a fully opaque cover over detected content and detect app switches for auto-start. Without this service, Android may limit overlays and auto-starting feature won\'t work.\nPrivacy: processing stays on-device only. KrazyHide has no internet permission.

KrazyHide needs Accessibility Service to:
• place a reliable full-opacity cover over detected content
• detect when selected apps open (auto-start)

How to enable:
1. Tap "Request permission".
2. In Accessibility settings, open "Installed apps" or "Installed services".
3. Select KrazyHide and turn it ON.
Required to keep KrazyHide running in the background. You\'ll also see controls to quickly stop or clear the cover.
Single app recording is not supported on this device. Using experimental full screen mode.
Single app recording is not supported on this device. KrazyHide will use the experimental full screen mode instead. This mode might not provide the best experience and quality.
Start KrazyHide automatically when selected apps are opened
Control how much content is blurred. Large pixels completely hide details, while small ones retain some features.
Something went wrong while starting detection.
Choose how KrazyHide should detect and hide content
Set how strict detection should be. Lower values detect more but can mistakenly trigger on normal content. Higher values reduce false alerts but may miss some content.
Control how visible the cover is. Higher opacity hides content more completely, while lower opacity keeps more of the screen visible.

Select the size of the pixels used to hide content. Large pixels hide details and blur the image, small pixels retain some general features and colors.\nLarge pixels are easier to process and consume less resources.
Normal mode uses full rectangular boxes, which might hide nearby elements. Detailed mode recognizes the exact shape of people and only covers them.\nDetailed mode requires more processing power.

For the best detection quality, choose \"Single app\" in the next capture picker.\n\nIf you really need \"Entire screen\", enable \"Full Screen Mode\" in Advanced settings before starting. Even then, the experience and quality may still be less optimal than \"Single app\".
The first load for each model type can take a few minutes.\nStarting detection is usually much faster next times
